lines.en_lit: Rather, it not to their immersion,

No; actually, to their impurity. was necessary for the latter clause However, one had relations with a menstruating woman more severe than transmits impurity through a bed or a chair, with a mild impurity. render foods and liquids impure.

Come hear that Rabbi Ḥiyya taught: the zav and the zava and the leper and the female leper, and one who had relations with a menstruating woman, and one who is ritually impure a corpse, their immersion is during the day. a menstruating woman and a woman after childbirth, their immersion is at night. a conclusive refutation

And before you remove him from the impurity of his house, remove him from the impurity a corpse. Rav Taḥlifa, father of Rav Huna bar Taḥlifa, said in the name of Rava: That is to say impurity corpse is permitted in the public.
Ravina said: Even you say that impurity a corpse is overridden in public Impurity a corpse is uncommon. impurity of his house is common,
It was stated impurity a corpse Rav Naḥman said: It is permitted in the public; And Rav Sheshet said: overridden in the public,

where there are ritually impure and pure that patrilineal family everyone, agrees that the pure serve the impure do not serve. When they disagree, to seek out and bring pure from a different patrilineal family
Rav Naḥman said: is permitted the public, and we do not seek out Rav Sheshet said: is overridden the public, we seek out
Some say Even where there are ritually pure and ritually impure that patrilineal family, Rav Naḥman disagreed and said even impure,
